meta.name: Lemon Tahini Sauce
meta.subtitle: Creamy sesame-lemon sauce for shawarma, falafel, roasted vegetables, and bowls

source.name: SSA Canonical Catalogs
notes: Tahini thickens when first mixed with lemon; thinning with water is expected.
Flavor improves after a short rest (garlic mellows, sesame rounds out).

summary: Classic lemon tahini sauce: tahini + lemon + garlic, loosened with water until silky. Great on shawarma, falafel, roasted veg, and grain bowls.

yield.notes: Makes about 1 to 1 1/4 cups depending on tahini thickness and added water.

optional.label: Blender (optional)
optional.notes: Useful for ultra-smooth sauce or if adding herbs. Whisking works fine.

notes: Contains sesame (tahini).
May contain traces of nuts depending on tahini brand/facility.

ingredients.label: Tahini (sesame paste), well-stirred
ingredients.notes: Stir tahini thoroughly before measuring (oil separates).

ingredients.label: Fresh lemon juice
ingredients.notes: About 2 lemons. Add gradually; tahini will seize (thicken) at first—this is normal.

ingredients.label: Garlic, finely grated or crushed
ingredients.notes: Use a microplane for smoothness; start with 1 clove and adjust.

ingredients.label: Cold water (to thin)
ingredients.notes: Add a little at a time until silky and pourable. You may need more or less depending on tahini.

ingredients.label: Ground cumin (optional)
ingredients.notes: Adds warm savory depth; common in shawarma-adjacent tahini sauces.

ingredients.label: Olive oil (optional)
ingredients.notes: Adds richness and sheen; not required.

ingredients.label: Parsley, finely chopped (optional)
ingredients.notes: For a green herb tahini sauce variation.

steps.title: Combine tahini, lemon, garlic, and salt
instructions: Whisk in lemon juice gradually until the mixture thickens (it may look stiff or grainy at first).
Add grated garlic and salt; whisk until combined.
checks: Sauce base is combined; thickening/seizing is expected at this stage.

steps.title: Thin to a silky sauce
instructions: Add cold water 1 tablespoon at a time, whisking well after each addition.
Continue until the sauce turns smooth, pale, and pourable (like heavy cream or ranch dressing).
Taste and adjust: more lemon for brightness, more salt for balance.
checks: Sauce becomes lighter in color and smooth as water is added.

steps.title: Optional seasoning and finish
instructions: If using, whisk in cumin for warmth and/or drizzle in olive oil for richness.
If adding parsley, stir in at the end (or blend briefly for a green sauce).

steps.title: Rest to mellow and set
instructions: Let the sauce rest 10 minutes before serving for best flavor.
If it thickens while resting, whisk in a splash of water to loosen.

notes: nutty
bright
creamy
savory
Use as a drizzle sauce for wraps and bowls.
Thin further with water for a dressing; keep thicker for spreading in wraps.

fridge.notes: Store in a sealed container. Sauce thickens when cold; whisk in water to loosen.

freezer.notes: Freezing can cause separation and gritty texture.

notes: Keep refrigerated; do not leave out for extended periods.
Use clean utensils to prevent spoilage.

batchNotes: Double batch keeps well and supports multiple shawarma meals.
If making ahead, reserve a little water for thinning after chilling.

notes: If your tahini is very bitter, add 1–2 tsp honey or date syrup (non-vegan) or a pinch of sugar to balance (optional).
For a thinner drizzle: add more water and a splash more lemon at the end.
Add 1/2 cup soft herbs (parsley/cilantro) and blend until smooth.
Thin with extra water as needed for drizzle consistency.
Whisk in 1–2 tsp harissa or 1/2 tsp cayenne.
Add water slowly—spice pastes can change thickness.
Omit garlic and add 1/4 tsp onion powder or a pinch of cumin for savory depth (optional).
Increase lemon slightly for brightness if desired.
